programme.title: Arthur: Francine's Pilfered Paper/Buster Gets Real
programme.desc: When Francine has a big paper over Thanksgiving, she copies from a website. There's nothing wrong with that, is there? Everyone loves the new Top Supermarket reality series on TV, except Arthur. Will it end his friendship with Buster?

programme.title: Arthur: Listen Up!/Arthur's New Old Vacation
programme.desc: When Francine's Bubbe passes away, Muffy tries to understand how she can help her friend. Then, when Arthur's family goes on the same vacation they take every year, he and Buster try to make it more exciting.

programme.title: Arthur: Happy Anniversary
programme.desc: Arthur's parents have been married for 10 years and it's time to celebrate! But the festivities are cut short when the van breaks down, stranding the whole family. Will the Reads miss their fancy dinner reservation?

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur and The Real Mr. Ratburn/Arthur's Spelling Trubble
programme.desc: It's the first day of third grade and Arthur's biggest fear has come true--his new teacher is the dreaded Mr. Ratburn. Then, it’s time for the all-school spellathon. Arthur is chosen to compete, and he finds facing Prunella and The Brain!

programme.title: Arthur: Night of the Tibble/Read and Flumberghast
programme.desc: The Tibble Twins are having their first sleepover! Will the Tibbles lock James in the dark basement? Then, a cupcake's been stolen! The primary suspect: Tommy Tibble. D.W. gathers a jury and demands that justice be served.

programme.title: Arthur: Room to Ride/The Frensky Family Fiasco
programme.desc: With Lance Armstrong’s support, Binky and his friends take to the streets to convince residents to vote for new bike safety lanes. Francine wants the Frenskys to be closer, but a weekly Family Night turns out to be harder than expected.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur Goes To Camp/Buster Makes The Grade
programme.desc: Arthur and his friends go to summer camp where it's strictly boys vs. girls. Next, Buster Baxter is everyone's favorite jokester. Unfortunately, though, Buster's grades aren't as good as his jokes, and Buster may have to repeat third grade.

programme.title: Arthur: The Great MacGrady, Parts 1 & 2
programme.desc: When Mrs. MacGrady is diagnosed with cancer, Arthur and his friends all react differently to the news. The kids must learn how to come together to help support each other and Mrs. MacGrady.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur's Treasure Hunt/The Return Of The King
programme.desc: When Buster digs up an ancient arrowhead, everyone else wants to hunt for buried treasures, too. And a field trip to the Medieval Fair pits Arthur's class against another led by Ratburn's super-tough mentor, Mr. Pryce-Jones.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop can't find anybody to play with her. When the others see her feeling sad, they decide to play whatever she wants to play. The dinos and kids are planning a camp-out at the park.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: A creepy spider inspires Barney to tell the story of “The Reluctant Dragon.”

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop is surprised by the first day of cold weather, Barney helps her see that winter is a wonderful time. On a hot summer day, Barney teaches BJ about all the activities you can do in the summer.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Rabbits - Listen
programme.desc: BJ is pretending to be a great detective. But all great detectives need a mystery to solve! One quickly comes his way when an animal nibbles on one of Baby Bop's healthy snacks.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Little Red Rock'n Hood - Differences
programme.desc: Baby Bop is having a hard time finding a story she hasn't heard before. Barney and the little dinos decide to tell her an old story with a new twist! They tell the story of "Little Red Riding Hood" in a whole new way...by singing it!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney and his friends are excited about a fishing contest. Barney tells Riff about a famous musician named Beethoven who wrote beautiful music even when he couldn't hear.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop, BJ and the children prepare for the Shapes Carnival in the park./When it starts to rain at the park, Baby Bop joins Barney and BJ inside the caboose.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop is disappointed when their trip to the zoo is postponed. Barney tells Baby Bop a tale about keeping your promise.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Let's Make Music
programme.desc: Barney, Baby Bop and BJ have fun learning about music, rhythm and musical instruments. Barney introduces many different instruments and BJ dreams of one day becoming a rock 'n roll star. They discover that music gives everyone a happy feelin.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Mean Machines
programme.desc: The hunt for the Eye of Sarnoth continues in the third of a four-parter with the Turtles in Groucho-like disguises. Shredder hooks up a fragment of the Eye to the computer Omnus.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Curse of the Evil Eye
programme.desc: Shredder is in control of the Eye of Sarnoth, enabling him to transmute matter. Shredder brings to life a dragon which soars through the air with shocked shellbacks Leonardo and Michelangelo aboard.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Case of the Killer Pizzas
programme.desc: The Turtles' favourite food, pizza, is also the source of their problem. Nasty, fanged creatures have been planted in pizza boxes! When the critters crack open their chrysalises, they become enormous.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Enter: The Fly
programme.desc: Shredder sends April a rare duko plant that renders her unconscious. The Turtles seek a rare leaf that can serve as an antidote.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Invasion of the Punk Frogs
programme.desc: Mutagen, sent by Krang from Dimension X, changes four Florida frogs into half-human creatures four and a half feet tall. Shredder, training them in his evil ways, names them Attila the Frog, Genghis Frog, Rasputin the Mad Frog and Napoleon Bonafrog.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Splinter No More
programme.desc: Michelangelo senses that their mentor is "mondo depressed" and Donatello finds a way of changing back Splinter the rat to Yoshi the man.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): New York's Shiniest
programme.desc: Shredder taps into New York's computers to steal plans for robots and creates an army of robots to destroy the Turtles and loot the city.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Teenagers from Dimension X
programme.desc: Neutrino teens Kala, Zak and Eask arrive on Earth and alert the Turtles to Shredder's latest plan to make "mindless zombies" of the Turtles.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Catwoman from Channel 6
programme.desc: A matter transporter results in strange happenings including TV reporter April O'Neil being put into a cat-like state and a tiger chasing the Turtles and Splinter, their mentor-rat.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Return of the Technodrome
programme.desc: To bring the Technodrome back to Earth, Shredder is instructed to take over the Niagara Falls hydroelectric station.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Beneath These Streets
programme.desc: While the Turtles, in disguise, are at a movie, Shredder interrupts a press conference to steal a new medi-laser.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Jon has difficulty sleeping, so a hypnotist puts Jon under hypnosis making him fall asleep from the noise of a horn, but he accidentally responds to Odie's bark instead.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Garfield accidentally gets sent into a parallel world; Garfield gets a taste of celebrity and glitter.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Garfield and Odie are framed for stealing barbecued ribs when they run away from Jon's twin girl cousins.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Garfield is suddenly sucked through a vortex into a parallel universe where cats' and humans' roles are reversed.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Garfield is enrolled on the Dr Whipple's Pet obedience course after Jon sneakily tapes him misbehaving.

programme.title: Dragon Ball Super
programme.desc: With a surge of power, Vegeta attacks Beerus; Beerus challenges Oolong to rock-paper-scissors to give Earth a second chance.

programme.title: Dragon Ball Super
programme.desc: After learning how to create the legendary Super Saiyan God, everyone realises that the transformation will require one more Saiyan than they have available, and now they need to figure out a solution.

programme.title: Dragon Ball Super
programme.desc: Holding the powers of a Super Saiyan God, Goku tries to face Beerus. Goku proves to be a true challenge, forcing the god to take him seriously.

programme.title: Dragon Ball Super
programme.desc: Seemingly in control of his powers, Goku escalates the battle. Beerus' power does not fade, and he continues to hit Goku.

programme.title: Dragon Ball Super
programme.desc: The battle between Super Saiyan God Goku and Beerus causes the universe to deteriorate. Old Kai notes that if the two continue to clash, the entire universe will be reduced to an empty void.

programme.title: Dragon Ball Super
programme.desc: The clash between Beerus and Goku nearly demolishes Earth, but the destroyer's energy cancels out Goku's attack and reduces Goku to a normal Super Saiyan.

programme.title: Dragon Ball Super
programme.desc: Goku continues the battle, but he lacks enough power to defeat the God of Destruction, and his energy runs out.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: After Gary comes to the rescue against Team Rocket, Ash seizes an opportunity…at long last, our hero gets to challenge his rival to their very first Pokémon battle!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Ash begins his journey in Johto, a region largely unexplored and populated with Pokémon entirely new to him and his friends.

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: On the road again, Ash and his Charizard soundly defeat an ambitious rookie Trainer named Casey. Upset, she runs away before Ash can comfort her—the perfect time for Team Rocket to strike!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: In a forest that's being destroyed by rampaging Pinsir, Ash has to enlist the help of the local Heracross to protect their home! But it turns out these Pokémon look tougher than they act…

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: On their way to Violet City, our heroes arrive in a valley where Donphan are used to harvest precious stones. Needless to say, the Team Rocket villains aren't far behind them!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: The forest can be a scary place at night, especially when you’re lost! Our heroes will need some help to get out of this mess. It's Hoothoot to the rescue!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: On their first stop in a city since New Bark Town, our heroes decide to help an insecure Bellossom dance troupe regain their confidence and perform in an upcoming exhibition!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Ash and the gang arrive in a town besieged by apparent copycat crimes mimicking a legendary master thief. The town’s hopes lie with these young Trainers...how can they stop this criminal duo and their Meowth accomplice?

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: When our heroes return an unhappy Snubbull to its wealthy Trainer, it sparks off a series of events that will change the Pokémon’s life forever!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: When Brock discovers a hurt and frightened Stantler in a nearby park, he decides to nurse it back to health. But with Jessie, James and Meowth on his case, can he keep Stantler safe?

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky sings a song about his bag and it appears. Dipsy sings a song about his hat and it appears. Laa-Laa sings a song about her ball and it appears.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children practise yoga. In Teletubbyland, the Teletubbies are very busy. Tinky Winky is marching with his bag, Dipsy is dancing with his hat, Laa-Laa is playing with her ball and Po is riding her scooter.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some girls spend the day picking and sorting vegetables in their nannies garden. Tinky Winky sets the table for Tubby Toast, but when he calls the other Tubbies, Noo-Noo tidies it all up.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky & Po burst out of the house to say Eh-oh and enjoy a big hug. Then they do it again. They join Dipsy & Laa-Laa to watch a little girl read the story of Jack & The Beanstalk.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies sit down for Tubby Toast. After they’ve eaten some more toast lands on their plates. They eat that, and some more toast lands again.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A black and a white cloud appear in Teletubbyland. The Teletubbies watch some children learn about the colour black.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A little girl goes to the shops to buy a Sari. The Noo Noo is tidying up the Teletubbies blankets, but Po doesn’t want the Noo Noo to tidy up her blanket.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po invites Tinky Winky to dance, and they dance all around Home Hill. Tamzin Griffin pretends to be Funny Lady and tells the story of the very proud crown.

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.title: Season of the Witch
programme.desc: His faith broken by years of battle as a crusader, Behmam, a medieval knight, returns to central Europe to find his homeland decimated by the Black Plague.

programme.title: The Big Wedding
programme.desc: A modern family tries to survive a weekend wedding celebration that has the potential to become a full-blown fiasco in this uproarious romantic comedy with an all-star cast.

programme.title: Drive
programme.desc: A Hollywood stunt performer (Ryan Gosling) who moonlights as a wheelman discovers that a contract has been put on him after a heist gone wrong.

programme.title: World Poker Tour: WPT Borgata Poker Open
programme.desc: The largest field in World Poker Tour history produces a final table with one of the biggest WPT personalities ever as hard drinking, fun loving, trash talking Jumbo Pressure Jeremy Brown tries to bully his way to a win on the WPT.

programme.title: World Poker Tour: WPT Festa al Lago - Part 1
programme.desc: Two time WPT champion Freddy Deeb returns to the bright lights of the World Poker Tour final table in search of an elusive third win and yet another million dollar payday.

programme.title: World Poker Tour: WPT Festa al Lago - Part 2
programme.desc: High stakes tournament poker can be a cruel game and this episode of the WPT demonstrates just how fickle Lady Luck can be.

programme.title: World Poker Tour: WPT Foxwoods World Poker Finals - Part 1
programme.desc: Madman Soheil Shamseddin returns to the WPT arena crazier than ever and ready to gamble his way to a WPT title.

programme.title: Orgy of the Dead
programme.desc: A couple driving to a cemetery looking for inspiration for a horror story encounters the dancing dead.

programme.title: Count Yorga, Vampire
programme.desc: In this cult-classic, a modern-day vampire terrorizes the City of Angels. With plenty of sharp-fanged villains and an eerie castle just minutes from the closest freeway, this bone-chilling horror story is one you can really sink your teeth into!

programme.title: Rollerball
programme.desc: James Caan stars as a rollerball champ in this fast-hitting, action- packed glimpse of a future where a brutal, no-holds-barred sport takes the place of war, and murder is just part of the game.

programme.title: Chaos
programme.desc: A brilliant thief has masterminded a series of high-stake bank heists, and for two detectives, the only hope for survival is finding an order to the chaos.

programme.title: Lionheart
programme.desc: Jean-Claude Van Damme stars as Lyon Gaultier, a member of the French Foreign Legion who deserts his post in order to come to America and help the family of his murdered brother. But crossing the U.S. without a penny to his name proves difficult.

programme.title: Beyond the Law
programme.desc: A former mobster turned businessman and a police detective must contend with a dirty ex-cop looking to bring the killer of his son to justice.

programme.title: Watching Over You
programme.desc: Single mom Sarah moves into the suburbs and starts dating a firefighter, only to find herself the target of an obsessive neighborhood watchman who aims to prove he’s the best man to protect her… even if he has to create the danger himself.

programme.title: Mary Higgins Clark
programme.desc: When Patricia is called to identify a body, she’s shocked to find her mirror image lying dead on a gurney. Determined to find out more about her surprise ‘twin’ and simultaneously, help her mother clear her father's name.

programme.title: Presumed Dead
programme.desc: An international crime novelist is the primary suspect in the disappearance of a young woman and it's up to Detective Mary Anne Cooper to piece together this bizarre missing person's case.

programme.title: Sugar Baby Murder
programme.desc: After graduating from college, 21 year old Bridget moves to a new town with her best friend Lindsay. Desperately needing a job to support her budding writing career, she takes a job as an EA to business tycoon Anthony Gloss.

programme.title: Jiu Jitsu
programme.desc: Nicolas Cage is back and this time he’s got a sword and an alien invasion knocking at his door! Humanity is on the brink of extinction as a being from another world returns to Earth to face off against the best warriors the human race has to offer.

programme.title: Velocity Trap
programme.desc: A cop framed for murder is the only one not in cryosleep when a team of thieves boards the armored treasury spaceship he's on to steal its load - 40 billion universal dollars - and then ram the ship into a comet to cover their tracks.

programme.title: Earthling
programme.desc: After a mysterious atmospheric event, a small group of people wake up to realize that their entire lives have been a lie. They are in fact aliens disguised as humans. Now they have to make a choice. Live amongst men, or try to find a way back home.

programme.title: The Amazing Transparent Man
programme.desc: A crazed scientist invents an invisibility formula. He plans to use the formula to create an army of invisible zombies.

programme.title: Alive
programme.desc: Two injured strangers awaken in an abandoned hospital with no recollection of who they are or how they got there.

programme.title: Odd Thomas

programme.title: A Night In The Woods
programme.desc: Brody(Scoot McNairy), his girlfriend Kerry(Anna Skellern) and their friend Leo(Andrew Hawley) go hiking in Dartmoor's Wistman's Woods, so named because of its legendary haunted past.

programme.title: Holiday Wishes
programme.desc: Britney, a rich, spoiled child, and Rachel, an orphan, switch bodies after a visit to the mall with Santa Claus. Each girl wakes up as the other - Britney is in the orphanage, and Rachel is at Britney's family's mansion.

programme.title: Christmas in Mississippi

programme.desc: Keppler is forced to face his past when an old face turns up asking him for a favour.

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: The CSIs investigate the disappearance of a high-school cheerleader and her boyfriend.

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: Grissom and Greg investigate a drive-by shooting on a limo.

programme.title: The Outer Limits: 1995

programme.title: The Outer Limits: 1995
programme.desc: When an accident puts Dr. Stein's (Mark Hamill) colleague, Rachel, in a coma, he uses his newly-created invention to reach her brain and tell her he loves her. However, Stein discovers that love takes strange turns when it enters the virtual world.

programme.title: The Outer Limits: 1995
programme.desc: After flashbacks of an alien abduction lead him to attempt suicide, Eddie Wexler (Michael O'Keefe) is institutionalized, with a doctor who seems intent on forcing him to relive his memories. Is Eddie insane? Or is the doctor more than he seems?

programme.title: The Outer Limits: 1995

programme.desc: Harm, Mac and Bud board an aircraft carrier in the Sea of Japan to investigate the accidental shooting down of a North Korean civilian airliner by Navy jets.

programme.desc: The SRU must rescue a young woman after she’s been abducted by a delusional man who insists she is his missing daughter.

programme.title: Flashpoint

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: A bitter child custody battle sparks a critical incident and the SRU must use a risky “rolling roadblock” maneuver to rescue the children.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One must locate and rescue a politician before a vengeful radio shock jock kills him on the air.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: The SRU are called in to resolve a gas station robbery but discover it’s connected to a deadly crisis on a cult compound.

programme.title: The A-Team

programme.title: The A-Team

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: An ambitious auto mechanic asks for help after a ruthless competitor attempts to ruin his business.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The team battles corrupt refuse collectors who are dumping toxic waste into the city's sewer system.

programme.desc: Liam and Renee investigate a dead pregnant woman missing her fetus. They discover a town that Da'an helped after a nuclear meltdown and a suspicious brother and sister who were part of an early experiment to combine human and Taelon DNA.

programme.title: Northern Exposure
programme.desc: Depressed Shelly flees her baby shower; a Hollywood agent wants Ed to change his script; Chris seeks his feminine side.

programme.title: Northern Exposure

programme.title: Northern Exposure

programme.title: MacGyver

programme.desc: University students Holly Dunn and Chris Maier are ambushed along railway tracks in August 1997. By 1999, a string of similar attacks leads police to Angel Maturino Reséndiz, who travels by freight train between murders across three states.

programme.desc: In 1995 Paris, Guy Georges, a serial killer, was linked to multiple murders through DNA and later confessed in court. He was sentenced to life in prison, leading to the establishment of a DNA database for offenders in France.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder

programme.desc: Was Presidential hopeful, Robert F. Kennedy really killed by disgruntled Palestinian Sirhan Sirhan as we’ve been led to believe? Or was he another in a long line of Manchurian candidates, a victim of a long standing mind control program.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Energy Healing
programme.desc: Several energy healers claim they are able to produce spontaneous miraculous healing through a laying on of hands. Claims of similar such healing by a man in a remote village in Brazil, known as John of God are also examined.

programme.title: Myth Hunters

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Is a powerfully rich cabal with a globalist agenda conspiring to destroy sovereign nations and impose authoritarian rule?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Execution of MLK and The Framing of James Earl Ray
programme.desc: In this re-examination of the assassination of civil rights leader Martin Luther King, Richard meets several researchers who provide evidence that the convicted gunman, James Earl Ray was not the real triggerman.

programme.title: Murder Maps

programme.title: Murder Maps: The Lady Killer

programme.title: Murder Maps: The Blackout Ripper

programme.desc: Project Gemini Advances - NASA refines the techniques needed for rendezvous. Cassini at Saturn - In the Cassini probe's final years of activity, its handlers are asking it to do more complex and risky assignments.

programme.title: War Story: The Road to Rome

programme.title: Secret War: The Scholar and the Fight for Greece

programme.desc: It's finally the first day of fourth grade and Arthur couldn't be more excited - until he learns Buster is in another class! Meanwhile, D.W. is nervous for her first day of kindergarten. Will Arthur and D.W. be able to make new friends?

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Let's Make Music
programme.desc: Barney, Baby Bop and BJ have fun learning about music, rhythm and musical instruments. Barney introduces many different instruments and BJ dreams of one day becoming a rock 'n roll star. They discover that music gives everyone a happy feelin.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Come Blow Your Horn!
programme.desc: Barney and the kids are excited because five of Mr. Boyd's brass-playing buddies are putting on a show in the park tonight! Angela is learning to play the trumpet and knows that it takes a lot of practice. BJ arrives and the group visits the caboose.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: A Wonderful World of Colors and Shapes
programme.desc: Picture this! When Barney shows off his new painting, his friends are inspired to create their own art show. Discovering how to create new colors and draw shapes, Barney and his friends put on a super-dee-duper art show

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney reminds Riff and BJ that Christmas is about more than getting gifts. Barney takes Melanie on a trip to the North Pole to deliver her letter in person.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop forgets to say "please" and "thank you". Riff shares his dream of becoming an inventor.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: A Fountain of Fun
programme.desc: While Barney and the children wait for Mr. Boyd's new fountain to be delivered, they have fun playing in and exploring the park.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Play It Safe!
programme.desc: It's always fun to share a day with Barney—but it's also important to learn how to "play it safe!" Baby Bop, wanting to be a good helper, has been attaching adhesive strips to her frustrated (and uninjured) brother, BJ.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Day and Night
programme.desc: With a little Barney magic, the children experience the differences between day and night!

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Beneath These Streets
programme.desc: While the Turtles, in disguise, are at a movie, Shredder interrupts a press conference to steal a new medi-laser.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Turtles on Trial
programme.desc: While the Turtles go on a talk show to prove their innocence to the world, Krang steals a robot and some Solarbenite from a military warehouse, with the intention of destroying the Turtles himself.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Attack of the 50-Foot Irma
programme.desc: While the Turtles are planning Irma's birthday party, Shredder steals an Exaboron Isotope from a meteorite crash. Krang creates an Exaboron-powered Enlarging Ray which causes six times normal growth, with the intention of using it on Shredder.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Maltese Hamster
programme.desc: Shredder gives futuristic laser weapons to mobster Tony Vivaldi and his underlings. Shredder wants to find the Maltese Hamster statue of him, which has inside it the formula for ultimate fuel for the Technodrome.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Sky Turtles
programme.desc: Krang creates a Gravity Altering Device which causes everyone in the city, including the Turtles, to start floating.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Old Switcheroo
programme.desc: Two huge electrodes at Cybertech Labs produce a startling effect on two long-time foes. The mind of the Turtles master Splinter winds up in the body of the evil Shredder - and vice versa, causing confusion among their associates.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Burne's Blues
programme.desc: Burne is out to prove to April that he can still dig in the trenches and find good stories after deriding her for failing to come up with anything good lately.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Fifth Turtle
programme.desc: On the occasion of a rare conjunction of the Earth, Moon, and Stars, Shredder steals some Capsidium Crystals.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Enter: The Rat King
programme.desc: April does a story on the rat problem the city is facing, and the Rat King kidnaps her. The Rat King is the leader of all the rats under his form of government known as the Ratocracy.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Turtles at the Earth's Core
programme.desc: The Turtles venture down to the Earth's core and discover a lost prehistoric world complete with dinosaurs.

programme.title: Dragon Ball Super
programme.desc: Goku continues the battle, but he lacks enough power to defeat the God of Destruction, and his energy runs out.

programme.title: Jade Armor
programme.desc: When the Crimson Lord locks fans in a comic convention, most think it's an escape room, but LJ knows it's no game. With nowhere to go and Theo and Alisha outside, turning Jade would blow her cover.

programme.title: Jade Armor
programme.desc: LJ gets hooked on a Jade Armour video game, obsessed with topping the leaderboard. While LJ is distracted, the Crimson Lord regrows his gauntlets.

programme.title: Jade Armor
programme.desc: A Shard transports LJ to an alternate universe. In this version of Ban Tang, everything seems amazing: Kai is LJ's doting boyfriend, and she isn't Jade Armour because Xin Qi is.

programme.title: Jade Armor
programme.desc: Xinyan has a plot to get all humans out of his way in one fell swoop and the Red Sceptre gets encased in ice. In a double double-cross, the monkey pits Jade and Crimson against each other.

programme.title: Jade Armor
programme.desc: On Blue Lynx's maiden heist, she takes a souvenir, a spool of thread, unaware of a Shard in it. With the power to bind those who wear it, LJ and Pearl wind up unlikely besties.

programme.title: Jade Armor
programme.desc: When a Shard makes emojis, social media filters, likes, and more appear in real life, the city becomes one big popularity contest.

programme.title: Jade Armor
programme.desc: A universal remote with a Shard in it allows Ban Tang to switch genres with the click of a button.

programme.title: Jade Armor
programme.desc: When Lan Jun puts on a pair of rose-coloured glasses with a shard, everything looks a lot brighter. Unfortunately, she also no longer sees the evil side of the Scarlet Lord.

programme.title: Jade Armor
programme.desc: Ban Tang isn't a ghost town, people are invisible. Black Tiger steals a crystal ball with an invisibility Shard in it to become the world's greatest thief.

programme.title: Jade Armor
programme.desc: Xin Qi needs a vessel like a genie needs a lamp. Tonight's strength-giving lunar event should help, but the Red Moon also causes Shards to rise.

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: When Brock discovers a hurt and frightened Stantler in a nearby park, he decides to nurse it back to health. But with Jessie, James and Meowth on his case, can he keep Stantler safe?

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Still on the road to Violet City, Ash encounters a wild Chikorita and tries to catch it. Chikorita proves to be a tougher opponent than he originally thought, and things only get worse when Team Rocket arrives…

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Professor Oak’s errand to deliver the mysterious GS Ball to a Poké Ball expert is interrupted when a Quagsire with an eye for round objects steals the GS Ball for a nocturnal ritual…

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Under yet another Team Rocket ambush, a young Trainer and her Ledyba help our heroes see them off. But, amidst the chaos, her Ledyba get separated from the group—where could they be?

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Our heroes make a rest stop in a Pokémon Center, which is run by a kind-hearted but misguided Blissey. Things only get worse when an old friend of Blissey's drops in unexpectedly…

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Finally reaching Violet City, Ash’s search for the Gym is halted by a trip to the local Pokémon Academy. Ash does well playing teacher, until one pupil takes off with Pikachu!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: It’s finally time for Ash’s first Johto League Gym battle, and he’s feeling confident. But Falkner’s Flying-type Pokémon prove to be tough competition! Can Ash take the win—and his first Gym badge?

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Back on the road after his first Johto Gym badge win, Ash and the gang find a cute Marill that won’t stop crying! Nothing our heroes try will stop it, and a Team Rocket raid certainly won’t help!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Team Rocket’s latest invention is bound to be a hit...until Togepi and its newfound Sentret friend take it out for a spin!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Ash hears of a training ground for wild Charizard and decides it's just the place to hone his own Charizard’s strength. But the tough lessons to be learned there will change Ash and Charizard’s friendship forever!

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: All four Teletubbies go under the slide. The Teletubbies watch some children go over a stile, under some trees and through a gate. When Laa-Laa goes for walk, Tinky Winky and Dipsy secretly follow her.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Teletubbies love to dance, but they love children even more. So everything stops when the Teletubbies watch a film all about a little boy's special friend: a pig call Winnie. There's time for Tubby Custard before the Tubby Bye Bye.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies are having some Tubby Custard when they stop to watch some children who are going strawberry picking.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Over the hills and far away, Po loves to ride her scooter fast and slow. The Teletubbies watch Emily ride in her trap, drawn by her pony Jester.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa walks up to say “Eh-oh”. Then she goes to find Po, Dipsy and Tinky Winky and brings them in to say “Eh-oh” too. The windmill turns and the Tubbies watch a group of children go paddling in the sea.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies want to go to sleep, but Tinky Winky finds Po already asleep in his bed. So he goes to look for somewhere else to sleep.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies line up to say 'Eh-oh' and then watch Matthew and Mark feed a baby calf. The Teletubbies play a game to see who can eat their Tubby Custard the slowest, but Po can't wait and eats her Tubby Custard very fast.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: It’s time for Tubby Toast. Po and Tinky Winky are so excited, they fall over. They join Dipsy and Laa-Laa to watch two children dig a big mud hole.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies giggle as they play their 'Through the Arch' game. The windmill stops them to watch a little girl and her dad go for a walk in the town.

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Without a Chute
programme.desc: After a tough auction, things get worse when the shop's landlord wants to talk to the guys. Ton tries to negotiate with him while Allen has to put an experimental powered parachute to the test to save the day's profits.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Cashville, Tennessee
programme.desc: Ton and Allen hit the road for Nashville, where they rumble with a band of tough, high-bidding bikers. The day explodes when the guys discover a 37 millimeter anti-tank gun and a cowboy's rodeo saddle.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Carolyn Goes Topless
programme.desc: A friendly competition with former employee Carolyn turns into a vicious battle for a 1968 Corvette Stingray, giving Carolyn the last laugh. Allen tests his Ninja skills with an antique sickle weapon.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Little-Ton Jones
programme.desc: Allen & Ton try to get the inside scoop on their competition by chatting up local auction buyers. The guys find some heavy gold-mining machinery from the 1950s & an amateur rocket that can travel faster than the speed of sound!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Win Lose or Joust
programme.desc: Ton and Allen race back from an auction to purchase a rare comic valued at over $100,000. The guys uncover a Samurai sword used for ritual suicide and Allen lives out his childhood fantasy of becoming knight.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Gone in 20 Seconds
programme.desc: Ton and Allen get taken down by their fiercest opponent, CJ, but a twist of fate keeps the guys in the game. They uncover a Geiger counter and NASCAR impact wrenches, while their accountant delivers some shocking news.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Animal Instincts
programme.desc: The first pump action shotgun - a Winchester Model 1897; a hunter's collection of prize big game, full-body taxidermy.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Don't Taze Me Bro
programme.desc: Two fierce opponents best Ton and Allen at the auction, leaving the duo in the lurch with a "trash unit" and little chance of breaking even for the day. And Ton gets tazed.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Ton-Geons & Dragons
programme.desc: While Ton soups up their new truck, Allen heads to an auction solo and encounters Carolyn and CJ. There he discovers LARP weaponry, a sword collection, and falconry gear, before Ton reveals their new ride: Betty.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Voodoo Moola
programme.desc: Allen & Ton hit a storage auction in Louisiana's Capitol City and quickly find themselves in trouble with the law! The guys uncover a pair of racing lawnmowers and then stumble on an antique barber chair from the early 1900s!

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 14
programme.desc: Aang, Katara, and Sokka discover a village of people who are all reliant on a fortuneteller in their everyday lives but they are so reliant that they are blind to the imminent danger they are all in.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 15
programme.desc: Aang feels insecure when Katara and Sokka come across something that reminds them of home. Because Aang thinks his friends might leave him, he hides an important message which only leads to trouble.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 16
programme.desc: Aang attempts to find a Firebending master to teach him while Katara learns that she possesses a special power.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 17

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 18
programme.desc: The kids reach the Northern Water tribe, and Aang and Katara look for a Waterbending Master as Sokka falls for a princess. Zuko must find a way to continue his quest for the Avatar.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 19
programme.desc: The Northern Water Tribe braces itself for an attack by Admiral Zhao's Fire Navy fleet. Aang struggles to defend the city. He wonders if there isn't a better way to use his Avatar abilities.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 20
programme.desc: Zhao's invasion breaks through the outer wall of the Northern Water Tribe's defenses, and he reveals a sinister plan. Aang goes to the spirit world for help.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 1

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 2
programme.desc: On the way to Omashu, the kids meet some nomads who show them a shortcut through the `cave of two lovers'. When they get lost in the caves, they must trust in love to escape.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 3
programme.desc: The kids return to Omashu so that Aang can learn Earthbending from King Bumi; however, when they get there, they are shocked to find the city is now in Fire Nation hands.

programme.title: Blown Away

programme.title: Next Goal Wins

programme.title: World Poker Tour: WPT Foxwoods World Poker Finals - Part 1
programme.desc: Madman Soheil Shamseddin returns to the WPT arena crazier than ever and ready to gamble his way to a WPT title.

programme.title: World Poker Tour: WPT Foxwoods World Poker Finals - Part 2

programme.title: World Poker Tour: WPT Foxwoods World Poker Finals - Part 3
programme.desc: An epic WPT final table comes down to two Texas gamblers in Soheil Shamseddin and Cornel Cimpan versus one young gun in the fearless Matt Stout.

programme.title: World Poker Tour: WPT Five Diamond World Poker Classic

programme.title: World Poker Tour: WPT Five Diamond World Poker Classic - Part 2

programme.title: Rollerball

programme.title: Bikini Nuns
programme.desc: A fearless trio of nuns is out for revenge. After being wronged by ruthless men, they trade their habits for high-powered weapons, taking justice into their own hands.

programme.title: The 39 Steps
programme.desc: A man in London tries to help a counter-espionage Agent. But when the Agent is killed, and the man stands accused, he must go on the run to save himself and stop a spy ring that is trying to steal top-secret information.

programme.title: Beyond the Law
programme.desc: A former mobster turned businessman and a police detective must contend with a dirty ex-cop looking to bring the killer of his son to justice.

programme.title: Wild Card

programme.title: The Big Fight

programme.title: Monkey
programme.desc: Monkey steals Heaven’s peaches, gains immortality, and battles officials. All are cast to Earth: Monkey trapped under rocks, Pigsy and Sandy cursed. Buddha decrees a holy man must fetch scriptures from India to save the world.

programme.title: Monkey
programme.desc: Kuan-yin seeks a holy man for the scripture quest, choosing young priest Hsüan Tsang, now Tripitaka. With Monkey’s magic staff, his powers, and a talking horse, their journey from China to India begins.

programme.title: Monkey
programme.desc: Tripitaka and Monkey start their perilous quest. A village terrorized by Pigsy leads to battle, then they face Sandy the cannibal water monster. Sandstorms and tiger ghosts threaten—will Monkey save his master from doom?

programme.title: Monkey

programme.title: Monkey
programme.desc: The youthful King kidnaps Tripitaka, desperate to outshine his father, the King of Ox. Monkey’s powers fail, Pigsy and Sandy are distracted—who will save their master from the fiery-tempered King of Youth?

programme.title: Ironside
programme.desc: The martial-arts murder of a Chinatown elder leads Ironside to a larcenous youth gang; guests Benson Fong, Frank Michael Liu, Mako.

programme.title: Ironside
programme.desc: An invitation to Ironside's class reunion contains one classmate's suggestion that her husband was murdered; guest Anne Francis.

programme.title: Ironside
programme.desc: A wealthy widow's (Kim Hunter) son (James Keach) is found dead shortly before his supposedly dead sister (Gretchen Corbett) reappears.

programme.title: Ironside
programme.desc: Attempts are made on a professor's (Michael Parks) life after he is mentioned on a tape left behind by a student who committed suicide.

programme.title: Ironside
programme.desc: A woman (Elizabeth Ashley) who hit Ironside's van suffers from an embedded bullet that shifted position.

programme.title: Cagney & Lacey: Act Of Conscience
programme.desc: While Lacey is on maternity leave, Cagney is assigned a controversial new partner who's past of turning in other cops who have participated in illegal activity makes the rest of the 14th precinct question how long he will stay among them.

programme.title: Cagney & Lacey: D.W.I.

programme.title: Cagney & Lacey: The Gimp

programme.title: Cagney & Lacey: Post Partum
programme.desc: Cagney (Sharon Gless) becomes involved in a case where a West Point cadet is charged with possession of cocaine and is refusing to help himself due to his honor code. Meanwhile, Lacey's (Tyne Daly) father pays her a visit after a 30-year absence.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: A lovely lady with an innocent smile, kind eyes, a charming personality? No one would suspect a woman like that would murder. These women don’t look or behave like killers.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Sometimes, when a man and a woman unite, a terrible urge for destruction can awaken in them both. Happy couples become deadly teams, intent on one goal: murder.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: A rifle in a shopping mall, a syringe of potassium chloride and a syringe of insulin, each in the hands of a mentally disturbed woman, lead to the tragic end of many lives.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Hearts of Stone
programme.desc: Deadly Women are not always the ones you expect. They are not the strangers lurking in the dark, they are a young mother, a supportive wife and a grandmother - all killers. Behind their loving masquerade lies a wake of family horror and pain.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: In some cases, the transformation from everyday woman to brutal killer is activated by a man. A boyfriend or husband can become the focus of a deadly obsession.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: While marriage is meant to be for life, it often ends in divorce. These wives took their marriages vow seriously: "till death do us part."

programme.title: The FBI Files: Hunter's Game
programme.desc: In the 1970's and 80s, people went to Alaska looking for a fresh start, to reinvent themselves, or to disappear for a while. Some of those disappearances weren't by choice.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Shattered Shield
programme.desc: In a city awash in narcotics the drug lords have all the power even over the police. Corruption in New Orleans grew like cancer, eating away at public safety and threatening to destroy the city. Crooked cops break the laws they were sworn to uphold.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: This is the tragic tale of Susan Smith, who shocked the nation in 1994 by fabricating a carjacking to cover up the drowning murders of her two young sons.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In January 2002, Ashley Pond and Miranda Gaddis disappear in Portland. Neighbor Ward Weaver, a suspect with a violent history, is later found guilty of their murders and sentenced to life in prison.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On June 27, 2005, Dennis Rader, known as the BTK killer, pleads guilty to ten murders he committed in Wichita, Kansas, from 1974 to 1994. After a journalist's article reignites the case, Rader is arrested and sentenced to ten life terms without parole.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On December 7, 1993, Colin Ferguson killed six and injured nineteen in the Long Island Railroad Massacre. Defending himself at trial, he bizarrely questioned his own victims and was sentenced to over 300 years in prison.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On June 28, 2005, Joan Porco attends the trial of her son Christopher, accused of murdering his father and attacking her. Despite her initial identification and later refusal to testify, Christopher is found guilty based on circumstantial evidence.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1991, Ray and Faye Copeland, the oldest death row couple in the USA, are convicted of murdering five drifters. Police find the bodies after a tip-off. Faye's death sentence is commuted to life in 1999.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In July 2000, Sarah Payne is abducted and murdered. Roy Whiting, a convicted sex offender, is found guilty and sentenced to life in 2001. Sarah’s case leads to the implementation of “Sarah’s Law” in the UK in 2011.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: Nathaniel Bar-Jonah, a paedophile, is convicted in 2002 for multiple crimes but not for the 1996 disappearance of Zachary Ramsay. Zachary’s body is never found, and Bar-Jonah dies in prison in 2008.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Face Of Tragedy
programme.desc: Forensic Sculpting: Forensic sculptors retrieve people from oblivion. Using clay and an intricate knowledge of anatomy, forensic arts place a face on an unidentified skull, recreating the victim's likeness, which often leads to his name.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show

programme.title: Murdertown

programme.title: Murdertown

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Malcolm Webster
programme.desc: The women in Malcolm Webster’s life seemed prone to bad luck. One died in a car crash. Another suffered a mysterious illness. A third, a keen sailor, found her life jacket punctured. How long before police realised this was more than coincidence?

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Stephen Searle
programme.desc: Stephen and Anne Searle were the grandparents at the helm of a large, close-knit family. But Stephen was having an affair, and Anne had been pushed to drink by her husband’s controlling behaviour. Neither was destined to enjoy a peaceful retirement.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Ann Marie Anastasi

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Hannegret Donnelly

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Iain Lawrence

programme.desc: It was WWII and London was rocked by the Blitz from the planes flying above. But down on the streets below another terrible danger lurked in the shadows. A serial killer was on the loose.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: In 1911, a gun battle on the streets of London, The Siege of Sidney Street the first breaking-news story in history.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: It was a crime which shocked Britain. In 1878, a widow was killed in her own home, the killer was her own servant. For the young servant did not only kill and dismember her employer – she stole her clothes, her property and her identity.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: Victorian double killer, the murderer of a young mother and baby - a woman named Mary Pearcey

programme.title: Murder Maps: The Brides In The Bath Killer
programme.desc: George Smith had many aliases. He needed them for his many wives who he would soon murder in order to claim the inheritance. Catching this chameleon would be a gargantuan challenge.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect: Miners: Fodder of the Industrial Revolution
programme.desc: The story of coal, inseparable from that of the Industrial Revolution, and of social protest, the rise of socialism, anarchism and trades unions.

programme.title: Wonders: Can We Make Life?
programme.desc: New tools allow researchers to use cells to create and edit their own DNA with more ease and less cost than ever before. NOVA Wonders explores the benefits and the burden of risk surrounding this controversial new technology.

programme.title: Naked Science: Inside The Mind Of Stephen Hawking
programme.desc: Stephen Hawking, though suffering from amyotrophic lateral sclerosis has developed a unique understanding of the universe.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: TORNADO
programme.desc: A tornado is a violently rotating column of air extending from the base of a thunderstorm down to the ground. Tornadoes are capable of completely destroying well-made structures, uprooting trees, and hurling objects through the air like deadly missiles.

programme.title: Naked Science: Magnetic Earth
programme.desc: The Earth hurtles through space protected by an invisible force field, known as a magnetosphere.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: HURRICANES
programme.desc: Hurricanes, Typhoons and Cyclones all the same thing with different names – what they also have in common is their destructive power! Hurricanes are large, swirling storms and winds from a hurricane can damage buildings or trees.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Stars Above
programme.desc: Stars are a bit like human beings. They can be warm or cold, they come in all kinds of shapes and sizes.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Planet X
programme.desc: The New Horizons mission continues deeper into space, it’s next target looming close.

programme.title: Inside Outer Space: Episode 3: Asteroids
programme.desc: Come with us as we explore and unravel the mysteries of what lies beyond our planet Earth. This is a series packed with space stories and information about our universe covering what we can see.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Orbit
programme.desc: The International Space Station is undoubtedly the jewel in the crown of low Earth orbit; observation post, research lab and class room.

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: Rebuilding Project Apollo - After the Apollo 1 disaster NASA resumes manned space flight. Orbiting Carbon Observatory 2 - Monitoring the Earth's Co2 levels in unprecedented detail. Zero Gravity Training - Parabolic flights.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Milky Way
programme.desc: This episode traces our view of the Milky Way, from the mythology of ancient sky watchers to the efforts of scientists to map it from the inside out.

programme.title: Moon Shots: Inside the Lost Apollo Archive
programme.desc: The world famous Apollo missions are some of the greatest feats ever accomplished by man. With rare archive footage and unprecedented access to life on board the Apollo spacecrafts, this stunning one-hour special brings scientific history to life.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Hubble's Renaissance
programme.desc: Before we can reach space, we must first be able to see it. Hundreds of years ago, the simple telescope was used to give us a seemingly closer vantage. Today, we use the awe-inspiring, and super powerful, Hubble Space Telescope.

programme.title: Meerkat Manor: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Pregnant Maybelline and her Aztecs are forced to fight their way out of trouble.

programme.title: Jungle Planet
programme.desc: The Florida Peninsula is dominated by an immense wealth of woodland, specifically wetlands and swamps.

programme.title: Wild Asia: Monsoon
programme.desc: A visually stunning and dramatic portrait of India’s mighty monsoon as it brings life — and death — to a thirsting land.

programme.title: Wild Asia: A Forest All Seasons
programme.desc: A year in the life of the temperate forest of Japan, where animals and plants must adapt to suit the variations of the climate in order to survive.

programme.title: Wildest Islands
programme.desc: Islands can be home to the most extreme examples of life, the wildest of people and the most dramatic landscapes. Natural selection fuels evolution in the most dramatic way

programme.title: Jungle Planet
programme.desc: The Madagascan rainforest is one of the strangest in the world. It is inhabited by lemurs whose howls at dawn and dusk fill the woodland.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Midnight Run
programme.desc: This fugitive has a bad reputation and a knack for running from the law. The pursuit has Dog and team traveling through an urban dystopia of suspicion, false leads and mistrust.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: The Hunt for Santa
programme.desc: Dog decides he needs to help his son Garry believe in Santa Claus again. So now the Dog, with a posse expanded to include his children and grandchildren, sets off on a hunt through the Colorado Rockies for Santa Claus himself!

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Call Waiting
programme.desc: Dog's target for today has been in trouble with the law since he was a juvenile. And he seems willing to use any trick in the system to avoid capture by Dog. In this match of wits, will Dog prevail?

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Kid Stuff
programme.desc: A fugitive from justice is on the lam and it's Dog's job to find him. When they hit the last known address they discover he already has pulled up stakes and left no forwarding address. The posse's one hope is to locate his girlfriend.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Easy Does It
programme.desc: There is a running joke in the posse that every time Bobby Brown asks them for help with a fugitive, he always promises them it's "an easy one," but the hunts end up being epic searches.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Ghost Rider
programme.desc: Dog believes in safety first, and today's hunt puts this philosophy to the test. The fugitive's problem with anger management increases the dangers of their already risky line of work. Dog warns the crew to be ready for anything as they head out.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Back in the Hood
programme.desc: Today, Dog's bounty is about to take him on a trip down memory lane and test his investigative chops. With each lead propelling him closer and closer to his childhood haunts, Dog uses old-fashioned detective skills to track his prey.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: One for the Road
programme.desc: Dog is a big-hearted guy. He's helping Mary Ellen, his friend and fellow bondsman, on a dangerous night hunt for a fugitive she is now regretting bailing out. He's also giving his nephew Justin a second chance to rejoin the team.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Bait and Switch
programme.desc: Things are seldom what they seem in the world of bounty hunting. When the fugitive's girlfriend offers to help Dog trap this dangerous felon, Dog knows that someone is being deceived. The only question is, whom?

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Si-Yonara

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Fowl Play

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Sweatin' Bullets

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Spring Pong Cleaning
programme.desc: After Korie decides the junk piles in the Duck Commander warehouse are beginning to reach critical mass, she calls on Willie and the guys to do a little company spring-cleaning.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Good Morning, West Monroe
programme.desc: After attending a local radio show as a guest, Willie sees an opportunity to host a show of his own.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Of Mattresses and Men
programme.desc: The Jase, Si and the guys from the duck call room take a field trip to a brand new local donut shop and quickly find out who amongst them can eat the most delicious treats.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Samurai Si
programme.desc: Willie receives a special gift from a Japanese business associate - a "priceless" samurai sword, which Willie keeps out of the hands of Jase and the guys.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Duck No, We Won't Go
programme.desc: Willie pushes his work force too far after implementing new "motivational work tactics," and the guys rise up against him and go on strike.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Drag Me To Glory
programme.desc: When NASCAR sensation Clint Bowyer pays a visit to the Duck Commander warehouse, Willie and the guys are fired up to spend the day hanging out with the professional racecar driver.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Duck Season Eve
programme.desc: Tomorrow is the opening day of Duck Season and the men are carrying on their tradition of camping on the Robertson Land the night before.

programme.title: Ice Pilots: Cold Start

programme.title: Ice Pilots: Shootout

programme.desc: Joe races a quirky antique snowmobile - with an airplane engine and no brakes. The water bomber crew struggles to get two planes out of Turkey. Cocky rampie Chris Staples tries to prove himself in the C-46.

programme.title: Ice Pilots: Aussie Ace

programme.desc: Facing Joe's wrath, Rod and Cory head to the UK to make urgent water bomber repairs. David tries to prove himself to Devan in the C-46 but ends up infuriating Devan. Will makes an ultimatum in Hay River.

programme.desc: This episode explores what would happen if a zombie outbreak ever became a reality.

programme.desc: Discover doorways to wormholes in time, their secret locations around the world, as well as who is trying to open them and why.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed: Time Travel

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: Episode 9

programme.title: Secret War: The Scholar and the Fight for Greece
programme.desc: Monty Woodhouse, a professor and a legend for blowing up the Gorgopotamos Bridge in Greece in 1942. In the British counter attack against Rommell at Al Alamein in North Africa it was vital to disrupt German supply routes, Greece.

programme.title: Secret War: The Dutch Disaster
programme.desc: Operation North Pole was a WWII German deception using captured SOE agents to mislead Britain. 47 of 50 agents were captured. Despite warnings, SOE ignored signs of compromise to maintain support, leading to a major intelligence failure.

programme.title: Secret War: The SAS Italian Job
programme.desc: In 1945, during the Italian Spring offensive, SOE agent Michael Lees and SAS officer Roy Farran led a raid on German HQs at Villa Rossi and Villa Calvi. Despite orders to cancel, Farran attacked with SAS men and partisans, killing many Germans.

programme.title: The Rise of the Nazi Party
programme.desc: Hitler finds his political destiny in post-war Munich. He quickly dominates a fringe party & builds it into the Nazi Party. He recruits infamous men to the cause including Herman Goering & Heinrich Himmler & they plot a coup known as the Beerhall Putsch.

programme.title: The Rise of the Nazi Party
programme.desc: In 1924, Hitler is convicted for High Treason after the failed Nazi power grab. In prison, he writes Mein Kampf, articulating his anti-Semitic and expansionist beliefs. Upon release, Hitler rebrands the Nazi Party.

programme.title: History's Greatest Aircraft: The Lancaster: Britain’s Frontline Bomber
programme.desc: The Avro Lancaster is the legendary British heavy bomber that carved its place in history with Bomber Command during World War 2. That conflict saw over seven thousand of these iconic aircraft come off the production line.

programme.title: History's Greatest Aircraft
programme.desc: Skyraider: The many variants of this single piston engine fighter bomber helped dominate the air to ground attack role in Vietnam, earning quite a reputation.

programme.title: WWII Air Crash Detectives

programme.title: U-Boat Wargamers: The Mastermind

programme.title: Guns That Changed The Game: The Gods Of War
programme.desc: The Gods of War: The industrial revolution changed it all when it came to cannons as they evolved from wholly inaccurate and immobile to deadly killing machines.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Endgame

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Turtles at the Earth's Core
programme.desc: The Turtles venture down to the Earth's core and discover a lost prehistoric world complete with dinosaurs.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): April Foolish
programme.desc: Emperor Aleister and his daughter Princess Mallory arrive from the small kingdom of Malacuria where there has been a recent discovery of a vast deposit of Lydium - 90, the most powerful source of energy found on Earth.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Attack of the Big MACC
programme.desc: At an army testing lab where a super laser is being demonstrated, the crowd is surprised by the arrival of MACC, a robot from the year 2399 that talks like a cowboy from watching old western movies on inter-dimensional trips.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Ninja Sword of Nowhere
programme.desc: Krang detects the existence of an alien metal that could help him conquer the Earth. It happens to be on a Ninja Sword the Turtles have purchased for Splinter as a National Sensei Appreciation Day gift.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): 20,000 Leaks Under the City
programme.desc: Krang is out to liquidate the Turtles by pumping water through the Earth's crust and into the sewer system. The super pump starts sucking up the entire ocean and soon the streets are covered with three feet of water.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Take Me to Your Leader
programme.desc: Leonardo cannot take the pressure of being the leader of the Turtles anymore and leaves. Meanwhile, Krang and Shredder use their Solar Siphon to drain all the energy from the Sun.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Four Musketurtles
programme.desc: Leonardo becomes a little soft in the shell after he is knocked on the head. He thinks he is D' Artagan and that the other Turtles are the Three Musketeers.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Turtles, Turtles, Everywhere!
programme.desc: The city's new state-of-the-art garbage disposal system's main computer DUMP 1000 is programmed by Shredder to seize the Turtles but starts scooping up every turtle in sight.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Cowabunga Shredhead
programme.desc: With the aid of the Holographic Synthesizer, Shredder creates an exact likeness of Michelangelo and programs it to invade the Turtles' lair and destroy them.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Invasion of the Turtle Snatchers
programme.desc: Donatello and Rocksteady are beamed up to a space ship - by little alien, Nikto, six thousand years old, traveling with his parents Klaatu and Barada.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Camera Bugged
programme.desc: The polarisoid, an alien tourist visiting Earth, causes all sorts of commotion with his camera which sucks everything it photos literally inside the camera, including City Hall.

programme.title: Jade Armor

programme.title: Jade Armor

programme.title: Jade Armor

programme.title: Jade Armor

programme.title: Jade Armor

programme.desc: Miss Gotto has been acting weird of late. // Who would have guessed that the Dancing Underpants Ghoulie would be haunting the old laundromat?

programme.title: Super Mario Brothers Super Show: Mario of the Deep
programme.desc: Mario, Luigi, Princess Toadstool and Toad have travelled to the underwater kingdom of Aqua Land in a submarine built from a bathtub in order to fight against BarraKoopa.

programme.title: Super Mario Brothers Super Show: Mario and the Beanstalk
programme.desc: Desperate for cash, the Marios trade the royal cow for bean seeds. Princess scolds them, and accidentally sneezes, blowing the beans into the yard.

programme.title: Super Mario Bros 3 Adventures: Mush Rumors/The Ugly Mermaid
programme.desc: A Family of Real World Humans stray off the vacation path and find themselves lost./In an underwater battle with King Koopa and his Doom Sub, the Brothers find Mev-tropolis.

programme.title: Super Mario Brothers Super Show: The Great BMX Race
programme.desc: To help Toad pay off his debt to Fry Guy, Mario and Luigi enter a bike race in hopes to collect the prize money. But what they don't know is that it's all a trap planned by Koopa.

programme.title: Super Mario Bros 3 Adventures: Crimes R Us/Life's Ruff
programme.desc: King Koopa and his evil horde orchestrate the escape of an infamous criminal./The mischievous Koopa Kids Hip and Hop snatch King Windbag's magic wand and zap him and Luigi.

programme.title: Super Mario Brothers Super Show: Mario and Joliet
programme.desc: In the Land of Romance, Mario, Luigi, Toad and Princess Toadstool, who had been called to the Land of Romance by a friend of Princess Toadstool, are currently trapped in a rapidly crumbling building.

programme.title: Super Mario Brothers Super Show: Pirates of Koopa
programme.desc: In Pirate Waters, located in Pirate World, Mario and Luigi are in the middle of peeling potatoes onboard a ship.

programme.title: Super Mario Bros 3 Adventures: The Venice Menace/Super Koopa
programme.desc: When a gondola suddenly warps into the Mushroom Kingdom, Mario and Luigi discover Venice./Kooky Von Koopa makes a magic pendant that gives King Koopa the power to become Super Koopa!

programme.title: Super Mario Brothers Super Show: Stars in their Eyes
programme.desc: In the depths of space, Mario, Luigi, Toad and Princess Toadstool are all aboard a yellow spaceship, which is currently traveling through a swarm of flaming meteors.

programme.title: Super Mario Brothers Super Show: Love 'em and Leave 'em
programme.desc: Mario and Co arrive in Rotunda Land, which is ruled by the uptight Queen Rotunda. After being caught, Mario unintentionally gets Rotunda to drink a love potion, causing her to fall in love with him, much to his dismay.

programme.title: Rogue Earth
programme.desc: In 2016, a wildfire ripped through Fort McMurray, in Alberta. It was Canada's worst natural disaster to then.

programme.title: Dust Storm
programme.desc: We investigate dust storm causes, and how to predict them and lessen their impact.

programme.title: House Hazards
programme.desc: The grass is not greener when the wheels of domestic disaster are set in motion by the House Hazards team.

programme.title: House Hazards
programme.desc: the House Hazards experts show how renovations can turn into wrecks in the hands of the not-so-handy.

programme.title: House Hazards
programme.desc: The House Hazards team simulate a few natural disasters and find you can never have enough weatherproofing.

programme.title: House Hazards
programme.desc: The House Hazards domestic scientists put a buzz kill on the handiness of table saws.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: Volcanoes can bury entire cities in clouds of ash and gas, or turn rivers into torrents of mud and debris.

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 122
programme.desc: Could a torture technique consisting of growing a bamboo through a victim's body have worked? Does dropping alkali metals in a water-filled bathtub result in a huge explosion?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 108
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a person slide down a steel cable using a pair of blue jeans? Does eye black help athletes see? Can a tree be cut down using a machine gun?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 99
programme.desc: Myths tested: Will a flaming trail of gasoline from a moving vehicle catch up to its tank and cause it to explode? Can a person be burned by a defibrillator if they are wearing a nipple ring?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 148
programme.desc: Can an object fired backward from a vehicle moving forward simply fall to the ground? Do the spy movie car tricks to escape a chasing car work?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 140
programme.desc: Can you mail a coconut without packaging? Can you make a candle out of earwax? Can you make someone urinate involuntarily by putting their hand in a bowl of warm water while they sleep?

programme.title: The Jerry Springer Show: Amazing Tales
programme.desc: Fred deceived his lover Jay, so now Jay wants his just desserts! Next, Terry's obsession over his stuffed panda is about to drive his girlfriend away! Then, Benny will tell his buddy that he secretly wears women's underwear.

programme.title: The Jerry Springer Show: Bizarre Betrayals
programme.desc: Heaven and Justin are here today to confess their infidelity to each other…about the same woman! Next, Joan wants to know why Mistress Flame prefers her line of work!

programme.title: The Jerry Springer Show: Adult Babies
programme.desc: Today you'll meet Taz and others who enjoy this bizarre lifestyle: adult babies. He and his other adult baby friends wear diapers and take milk from a bottle.

programme.title: The Jerry Springer Show: I'm Taking Your Man
programme.desc: Jennifer refuses to let her ex-boyfriend, Marcus, go. She says she'll get him back no matter what it takes. Next, Eddie left Glinda and their two-year-old son 16 years ago. Today, Glinda tells Eddie that she wants him back.

programme.title: The Jerry Springer Show: Leave Your Lover
programme.desc: Two women on a war path meet today! Sarah's ready to fight to get her sister-in-law Bobbie out of her life. Next Guy confronts his wife Tanisha's lover Merc!

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: You Failed Five Times?
programme.desc: Michelle's current boyfriend Everett can't run from Robert's allegations about his daughter. Will Everett's lie detector test results prove his innocence?

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The remaining chefs face a gruelling three-part challenge that will determine who receives the coveted black jacket.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: A luxurious overnight trip to Las Vegas is at stake as the remaining chefs compete in a head-to-head challenge.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The remaining hopefuls are challenged to create a unique dining experience for a large group of well-known chefs.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The final two chefs work on their menus for a star-studded final dinner service. Only one chef will walk away victorious.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: Eight former contestants are back to take on eight keen rookies. First up is the signature dish challenge, with the veterans trying to create the rookies' meals and trying to impress Gordon early on.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: Chef Ramsay announces that he is taking his risotto off of the Hell's Kitchen menu, and the contestants are tasked with preparing their own risotto dish in the hopes of replacing the old one for the remainder of the season.

programme.title: The Game: The Rules Of The Game
programme.desc: As a favor to Derwin, Melanie attends the first Saber Sunbeam meeting of the season, only to learn that unlike the wives, football players "girlfriends" are not considered legitimate and are treated as such by the other wives.

programme.title: The Game: Gifted
programme.desc: When Melanie sends gifts to the football team on behalf of Derwin in honor of the first game, Derwin gets teased and his teammates Jason and Malik feel pressured to reciprocate. A gift giving competition ensues.

programme.title: The Game: How Tasha Got Her Groove Back
programme.desc: When Malik tells Tasha that he is having trouble with the offensive coordinator, Kenny, he asks her to speak with him.

programme.title: The Game: Brittany's Super Sweet Sixth
programme.desc: When Kelley's daughter tells her she wants an extravagant birthday party like the rest of her friends, Kelly approaches Jason for money and he refuses.

programme.title: The Game: Rift And Separate
programme.desc: A minor argument escalates into a major locker room rigt between Jason and Malik that affects their playing relationship on the field.

programme.title: The Game: Mi Casa Es Su Casa
programme.desc: When Tasha returns early from a trip she finds Malik throwing a wild party at their house and shuts it down.

programme.title: The Game: The Trey Wiggs Episode
programme.desc: When Derwin's image consultant tells Derwin her idea to pair him with football player Trey Wiggs, his former college teammate, he objects, saying he and Trey were more like rivals than teammates.

programme.title: The Game: The Trey Wiggs Fallout Episode
programme.desc: When Melanie learns that Derwin is still upset about the bad publicity caused by her continuing friendship with an old football rival, she tries to find various ways to make it up to him.

programme.title: The Game: There's No Place Like Home
programme.desc: With Christmas approaching, the Sabers lose their final game and Melanie looks forward to going home to see her family and spending some quality time with Derwin.

programme.title: Moesha: Break It Down
programme.desc: Frank takes Hakeem to the father/son “lock-in” at the local church, to encourage open communication and trust between fathers and sons.

programme.title: Moesha: Rhythm Dues
programme.desc: Mo encourages CPQ to submit their latest song to the local radio station for their “play it or stuff it” segment. When the song is played, it is well received. Q has written lyrics over a Morris Day song, and Morris shows up at “The Hole” where he plays.
